behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content". Slot B: handoff boundary (diff retrieval/parsing -> review-input builder). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a pure parser test can pass while wiring still feeds full-file blobs into the analyzer. Integration-brake: no real OS/network required for this behavior; no model-output correctness required; no multi-agent sequence. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text does not require real GitHub transport, only correct module handoff of scoped content.
behaviors.test_description: Run a test wiring PR-diff loader + scope builder + analyzer-input formatter with fixture PR data containing both changed and unchanged lines; assert produced review input contains only changed hunks/line ranges for each file.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request". Slot B: real-infra boundary (GitHub diff-position semantics over real HTTP API). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, mocked API accepts positions that GitHub would reject or remap, hiding true placement bugs. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text is about placement mechanics, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments using computed diff positions and verify via GitHub API/UI metadata that each comment lands on the intended file/hunk/line in the PR diff.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "agent identifies at least one real defect" with a "known introduced bug". Slot B: model-output boundary (LLM defect detection quality). Slot C: if lowered to System, deterministic/stubbed findings can pass even when the real model misses the bug. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow because this behavior targets a single model judgment outcome (finding quality), not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real analyzer model on a seeded buggy PR diff (or equivalent real review input) across repeated trials; assert detection rate for the known bug is above threshold (e.g., at least 1 hit in N runs, or stronger SLO as defined).

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run". Slot B: handoff/state boundary (inline-post response IDs -> summary assembler). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, isolated formatter checks miss cross-module run-ID/ID-set mismatches. Integration-brake: no real OS/network required to catch mapping logic; no model-output dependence; no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text is correspondence within run state, reproducible with in-process fakes.
behaviors.test_description: Wire inline-comment posting adapter (fake returning realistic IDs) with summary builder in one run context; assert each summary finding references an ID in the exact returned set for that run and no foreign/stale IDs are present.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting". Slot B: handoff/state boundary (collector -> payload builder). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, single-function list checks can pass while orchestration filters/dedup stages drop items in transit. Integration-brake: no real OS/network required for the drop bug; no model-output correctness required; no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: rejected System because the failure can be reproduced via local module wiring and fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestration with a multi-finding inline result set through all local transformation steps up to summary-post payload creation; assert one-to-one preservation (or explicit documented merge rules) with no unintended loss.

behaviors.description: The automation sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real pull request when provided a fixed precomputed finding set.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "succeeds for a real pull request" with sequence "read diff, post inline comments, post summary". Slot B: real-infra boundary (real GitHub API/auth/rate-limit/HTTP behavior). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, mocked transports hide real API failures, permission issues, and ordering/consistency quirks. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text explicitly fixes findings (no model-output dependence).
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end on a real test PR using precomputed findings (no live model step); verify diff read succeeds, all inline comments are posted, summary is posted, and resulting artifact counts/IDs match expected.

behaviors.description: The full live run with model-generated findings (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "full live run with model-generated findings" and sequence "read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary". Slot B: multi-step agentic boundary (agentic analyze step feeding downstream posting pipeline on real PR infra). Slot C: if lowered to Agentic, you can validate model quality in isolation but miss end-to-end sequencing failures where generated findings break posting/summary stages. Rejection-gate: rejected pure System because behavior text requires correctness of model-generated content in the live sequence, not just transport execution.
behaviors.test_description: Run full unattended pipeline on a real test PR with real model analysis enabled; assert run reaches terminal success, inline comments and summary are posted, summary references posted inline IDs, and no human retry/recovery step is needed.
